name: v2_query_aware_gqa_r0

note: v2 query_aware GQA @ 25% (keep 432/576), text<->patch cosine similarity (max pool, SparseVLM-style). vs proxy r25 (0.555), v1 r25 (0.570). NOTE: n=50 directional read on TextVQA showed query_aware UNDERPERFORMS proxy (0.380 vs 0.500 matched) -- the full n=200 GQA run confirms whether this holds on scene/QA too or whether GQA (less OCR-dependent) behaves differently.

name: v2_query_aware_textvqa_r50
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.50 --selector query_aware --benchmark textvqa --subset eval/subsets/textvqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_v2/textvqa_r50_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v2 query_aware TextVQA @ 50% (keep 288/576) -- THE KEY OCR TEST. Full n=200 confirms the n=50 directional read (query_aware=0.380 vs proxy=0.500 on the SAME first-50). vs proxy r50=0.530, v1 r50=0.445, FastV r50=0.555. DIRECTIONAL READ IS NEGATIVE (see method-design.md §10): simple cosine text<->patch similarity does NOT recover OCR -- the proxy's hidden-state saliency is a stronger text-region locator. If the full run confirms <proxy, query_aware v2-step-1 needs a better scoring function (e.g. a learned projection, or SparseVLM's full attention-weighted aggregation, not raw cosine).
